issues.display_title: Issue #4105: Refactor PhotographerPortfolio.jsx to Reduce File Size and Duplicate Logic
issues.main_description_html:
Problem

The PhotographerPortfolio.jsx file has grown too large (1000+ lines). It currently contains duplicated UI components and state management logic for four portfolio types: Gothru, Google Street View, Panorra, and Matterport.

Proposed Solution

We need to decouple the UI from the business logic and eliminate redundancies:

  1. Extract UI Component: Create a reusable PortfolioAccordionItem.jsx component to handle the duplicated accordion structure.
  2. Extract Business Logic: Create a custom hook usePortfolioManager.js to manage all state (fetching, saving, deleting, and reordering) for the portfolios.
  3. Refactor Main File: Update PhotographerPortfolio.jsx to map through an array of configurations and render the extracted component using the custom hook, significantly reducing the file size.

issues.display_title: Issue #4108: Fix AI Blur Modal: Rename to "Free AI Tokens", Conditional Display & Correct "Free Tokens Applied" Label
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Follow-up corrections to the AI Blur order-summary modal (the free/paid AI token breakdown from #4083), based on feedback on the live UI.

In the current modal (example: Order Summary for 48 Images, 19 free AI tokens available), two labeling/visibility issues were flagged:

  1. Wrong label — "Remaining AI Tokens" should be "Free AI Tokens". The balance row at the top of the summary currently reads "Remaining AI Tokens"; it should read "Free AI Tokens" since it represents the free token bucket.
  2. Conditional display. This Free AI Tokens row (and its applied sub-row) should only be displayed when the user actually has free tokens. If there are no free tokens, do not render the row at all.
  3. Wrong sub-row label — "Paid Credits Applied" should be "Free Tokens Applied". In the screenshot, the deduction sub-row shows "Paid Credits Applied -19 AI Tokens", but those 19 deducted tokens are the free tokens being consumed — so this sub-row must be labeled "Free Tokens Applied" (green), not "Paid Credits Applied".

Scope

  • Rename the "Remaining AI Tokens" label to "Free AI Tokens" in the AI Blur order summary.
  • Render the Free AI Tokens balance row only when free tokens > 0; hide it entirely when there are none.
  • Ensure the deduction of free tokens is labeled "Free Tokens Applied" (not "Paid Credits Applied"), and only shown when free tokens are actually applied.
  • Keep "Paid Credits Applied" labeling reserved for actual paid/subscription credit deductions.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The top balance row reads "Free AI Tokens" (not "Remaining AI Tokens").
  2. When the user has 0 free tokens, the Free AI Tokens row and its applied sub-row are not displayed at all.
  3. When free tokens are consumed, the sub-row is labeled "Free Tokens Applied" (green) with the correct negative amount.
  4. The "Paid Credits Applied" label only appears for genuine paid/subscription credit deductions.
  5. Totals (Total Cost, Tokens required, Saved for Later) remain correct and unchanged.

Reference: docs/brains/2026-06-17-ai-blur-free-token-balance-design.md and docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md §5.2 (Token micropayments). Refines #4083; file: src/pages/Tour/Detail/Blur/Components/AIBlurModal.jsx.

Note from Developer

This has already been implemented under the following conditions:

  • If a user has free tokens available, the system displays "Free AI Tokens" and "Free Tokens Applied".
  • If the free tokens are used up, these labels are not shown.
  • 1 tokens = 20 AI tokens. If a user only uses part of their tokens, the remainder stays available for later use.
  • Because of this, "Remaining AI Tokens" and "Paid Credits Applied" are shown to indicate that there are still leftover AI tokens from a previous purchase.
2026-06-30.14-43-03.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4109: Skip Whitelabel URL for TourMkr Stats on Internal/Owner Accounts
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Feedback note from an internal/owner account (user_id: 2):

"As a note, for user_id=2 (me) there is no need to use the whitelabel URL for TourMkr stats."

Currently the dashboard builds the TourMkr stats links using the whitelabel URL. For this account (and similar internal/owner accounts), the whitelabel URL is unnecessary — the stats should use the direct / default (non-whitelabel) URL instead.

Note: Google Stats whitelabel handling is done on the API side, not the dashboard. See BE task: GoThruMedia/GoThru-API-New#1469 — Skip Whitelabel URL for Google Stats (API side) for Internal/Owner Accounts

Scope

  • For TourMkr stats, do not apply the whitelabel URL for user_id: 2 (and any other accounts that fit the agreed rule).
  • Use the direct/default stats URL for these accounts instead of the whitelabel domain.
  • Leave whitelabel behavior unchanged for all other accounts that should keep it.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. For user_id: 2, the TourMkr stats link/stats no longer use the whitelabel URL — they use the direct/default URL.
  2. Whitelabel URL behavior remains intact for all other (non-exempt) accounts.

❓ Open question for confirmation: Should this exemption apply only to user_id: 2, or to a broader group (e.g. all internal/admin/owner accounts, or accounts without whitelabel configured)? Please confirm the exact condition before implementation.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §5.4 (white-label / agency) and §4 (analytics / stats integrations).
